name: PySpark (Apache Spark Python API)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); cluster costs for cloud deployment

auth.notes: Local mode: no auth; Hadoop cluster: Kerberos; Cloud: provider credentials (Databricks, EMR, GCP Dataproc)

performance.notes: Startup overhead 5-30s for JVM/cluster; excellent throughput at petabyte scale

agent_readiness.notes: Python API for Apache Spark — distributed computing for big data. DataFrame API (Spark SQL). Spark Streaming (structured). MLlib for distributed ML. GraphX. Runs on Hadoop YARN, Kubernetes, local mode. Databricks (cloud Spark) widely used. Handles petabyte-scale ETL. Python overhead via Py4J JVM bridge. Spark Structured Streaming for real-time. Essential for enterprise data engineering pipelines.

gotchas: JVM startup adds 10-30s overhead (not for interactive/low-latency)
Python → JVM serialization overhead can be significant
Cloud costs at scale can be high
best_when: Enterprise data lake processing on Databricks/EMR
Structured Streaming for real-time large-scale data
avoid_when: Data that fits in single machine (Polars/Dask much simpler)
summary: PySpark is Apache Spark's Python API for distributed big data processing at petabyte scale. DataFrame SQL API, structured streaming, and MLlib. Runs on Databricks/EMR/GCP Dataproc. JVM-based with Python bridge. Essential for enterprise data lake ETL pipelines. 10-30s startup overhead and complexity overhead — use Polars/DuckDB for data that fits on one machine.
